# PetClinic on Kubernetes — Full CI/CD Guide (Jenkins + ArgoCD + SonarQube + Trivy)

End-to-end guide to deploy the PetClinic app on a local `kind` cluster with a complete
GitOps pipeline: **Jenkins** (build + push + scan) → **SonarQube** (code quality) →
**Trivy** (image vulnerability scan) → **ArgoCD** (auto-deploy on manifest change).

```
git push (main)
   │
   ▼
Jenkins (pollSCM, detects commit)
   │
   ├─ Checkout
   ├─ SonarQube Analysis + Quality Gate
   ├─ Kaniko: build image → push to DockerHub
   ├─ Trivy: scan pushed image for vulnerabilities
   ├─ sed: bump image tag in app-k8s.yaml
   └─ git commit + push (manifest bump)
          │
          ▼
     ArgoCD (auto-sync, detects new commit)
          │
          ▼
     kubectl apply → rolling update on petclinic Deployment
```

---

## 0. Prerequisites

- Docker Desktop (Windows/Mac) or Docker Engine (Linux), with **at least 6–8GB memory**
  allocated to the Docker VM — this stack (Jenkins + ArgoCD + SonarQube + app) is heavy
  for a default kind node. This was the #1 source of problems in this build (see
  Troubleshooting section).
- `kind`, `kubectl` installed and on PATH.
- A public DockerHub repo (e.g. `medanyyy/pet-clinic`) and an **access token with
  Read & Write scope** (Account Settings → Security → Personal Access Tokens).
- A GitHub repo containing the app source, Dockerfile, `Jenkinsfile`, and `app-k8s.yaml`.
- A GitHub Personal Access Token with `repo` (or fine-grained `Contents: Read and write`)
  scope, for Jenkins to push manifest updates back to the repo.

**Never paste real tokens/passwords into chat, tickets, or anywhere public.** Every
credential pasted during this build had to be revoked and rotated — see Troubleshooting.

---

## 1. Create the cluster and namespace

```bash
kind create cluster
kubectl cluster-info
```

```yaml
# namespace.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: petclinic
```

```bash
kubectl apply -f namespace.yaml
```

---

## 2. Deploy the PetClinic app

```yaml
# app-k8s.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: petclinic

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: petclinic
  namespace: petclinic
  labels:
    app: petclinic
spec:
  replicas: 2
  revisionHistoryLimit: 5

  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1

  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: petclinic

  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: petclinic
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: petclinic
          image: medanyyy/pet-clinic:1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "512Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "1Gi"
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 1000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: petclinic
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: petclinic
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 8080
```

```bash
kubectl apply -f app-k8s.yaml
```

This file lives in the **root of the GitHub repo** — Jenkins bumps the image tag in it,
and ArgoCD watches it for deployment.

---

## 3. Jenkins — RBAC + StatefulSet (persistent, with plugins baked in)

Jenkins needs permission to spawn agent pods (Kaniko, kubectl, SonarQube scanner, Trivy)
in-namespace, and needs to survive restarts (plugin installs from the UI force a
restart) — so it runs as a **StatefulSet with a PVC**, not a bare Pod or Job.

```yaml
# jenkins-rbac.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: jenkins
  namespace: petclinic

---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: jenkins
  namespace: petclinic
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods", "pods/log", "pods/exec", "secrets", "configmaps", "persistentvolumeclaims"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete", "update", "patch"]
  - apiGroups: ["apps"]
    resources: ["deployments"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]

---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: jenkins
  namespace: petclinic
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: jenkins
    namespace: petclinic
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: jenkins
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
```

```yaml
# jenkins-statefulset.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: jenkins-home
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: jenkins
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  serviceName: jenkins
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: jenkins
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: jenkins
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: jenkins
      initContainers:
        - name: install-plugins
          image: jenkins/jenkins:lts-jdk17
          command:
            - jenkins-plugin-cli
            - -d
            - /var/jenkins_home/plugins
            - --plugins
            - workflow-aggregator
            - git
            - github
            - docker-workflow
            - credentials-binding
            - kubernetes
            - kubernetes-cli
            - kubernetes-credentials-provider
            - pipeline-utility-steps
            - timestamper
            - ansicolor
            - sonar
          volumeMounts:
            - name: jenkins-home
              mountPath: /var/jenkins_home
      containers:
        - name: jenkins
          image: jenkins/jenkins:lts-jdk17
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
            - containerPort: 50000
          env:
            - name: JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Djenkins.install.runSetupWizard=false"
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "512Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "2Gi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: jenkins-home
              mountPath: /var/jenkins_home
      volumes:
        - name: jenkins-home
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: jenkins-home

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: jenkins
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: jenkins
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
    - name: jnlp
      port: 50000
      targetPort: 50000
```

```bash
kubectl apply -f jenkins-rbac.yaml
kubectl apply -f jenkins-statefulset.yaml
kubectl port-forward svc/jenkins -n petclinic 8080:8080
```

Browse `http://localhost:8080` — dashboard opens directly (`runSetupWizard=false`),
plugins from the initContainer list are already installed, no login screen.

> `-Djenkins.install.runSetupWizard=false` **skips the setup wizard entirely**,
> meaning no login prompt and no interactive plugin picker. Plugins must instead be
> supplied via the initContainer's `--plugins` list — installing via the UI afterward
> will restart the container (see Troubleshooting: "Plugin install restarts pod").

### 3.1 Configure Jenkins' Kubernetes Cloud

Jenkins runs *inside* the cluster it needs to spawn agent pods in:

**Manage Jenkins → Clouds → New cloud → type Kubernetes**
- Kubernetes Namespace: `petclinic`
- Jenkins URL: `http://jenkins.petclinic.svc.cluster.local:8080`
- Leave Kubernetes URL / credentials blank — Jenkins auto-detects in-cluster config
  from its own ServiceAccount.
- Test Connection → should succeed given the RBAC above.

### 3.2 Add credentials

**Manage Jenkins → Credentials → (global) → Add Credentials**

| ID | Kind | Value |
|---|---|---|
| `github-cred` | Username/Password | GitHub username + PAT (Read/write repo scope) |
| `sonar-token` | Secret text | SonarQube user token (see step 5) |

---

## 4. DockerHub secret for Kaniko (image push)

Kaniko builds and pushes images **without needing a Docker daemon** — required since
kind nodes run containerd, not Docker, so mounting `/var/run/docker.sock` won't work.

```bash
kubectl create secret docker-registry dockerhub-cred \
  --docker-server=https://index.docker.io/v1/ \
  --docker-username=medanyyy \
  --docker-password='<DOCKERHUB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN>' \
  -n petclinic
```

Verify (PowerShell):
```powershell
$json = kubectl get secret dockerhub-cred -n petclinic -o jsonpath='{.data.\.dockerconfigjson}'
[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String($json))
```

> **Kaniko expects the mounted file to be named `config.json`**, but a
> `docker-registry` secret stores it under the key `.dockerconfigjson`. Without
> remapping this, Kaniko silently fails auth (see Troubleshooting). Fixed in the
> Jenkinsfile's pod template below via `items` remapping.

---

## 5. Deploy SonarQube

```yaml
# sonarqube.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: sonarqube-data
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sonarqube
  namespace: petclinic
  labels:
    app: sonarqube
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sonarqube
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sonarqube
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: busybox
          command: ["sh", "-c", "sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: sonarqube
          image: sonarqube:community
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9000
          env:
            - name: SONAR_ES_BOOTSTRAP_CHECKS_DISABLE
              value: "true"
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "1Gi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "2Gi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sonarqube-data
              mountPath: /opt/sonarqube/data
      volumes:
        - name: sonarqube-data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: sonarqube-data

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sonarqube
  namespace: petclinic
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: sonarqube
  ports:
    - port: 9000
      targetPort: 9000
```

```bash
kubectl apply -f sonarqube.yaml
kubectl port-forward svc/sonarqube -n petclinic 9000:9000
```

Login `http://localhost:9000` with `admin` / `admin` (forces password change).

**Generate a token for Jenkins:** Administration → Security → Users → `admin` →
Tokens → generate → store as the `sonar-token` credential in Jenkins.

**Configure the server in Jenkins:** Manage Jenkins → System → SonarQube servers →
Add: Name `sonarqube`, URL `http://sonarqube.petclinic.svc.cluster.local:9000`,
credential `sonar-token`.

**Webhook (for Quality Gate check):** Administration → Configuration → Webhooks →
`http://jenkins.petclinic.svc.cluster.local:8080/sonarqube-webhook/`

---

## 6. Install ArgoCD

```bash
kubectl create namespace argocd
kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/install.yaml
kubectl get pods -n argocd -w
```

Admin password:
```bash
kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret -o jsonpath="{.data.password}"
```
```powershell
# PowerShell decode
$pw = kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret -o jsonpath="{.data.password}"
[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String($pw))
```

```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/argocd-server -n argocd 8081:443
```
Browse `https://localhost:8081`, log in as `admin`.

### 6.1 AppProject + Application

Apply these **directly with kubectl** — do **not** commit them into the same repo
path that the Application syncs (see Troubleshooting: "namespace argocd not
permitted").

```yaml
# petclinic-project.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: AppProject
metadata:
  name: petclinic-project
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  description: PetClinic project
  sourceRepos:
    - 'https://github.com/Mahmoud-Medany/pet-clinic.git'
  destinations:
    - namespace: petclinic
      server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
  clusterResourceWhitelist:
    - group: '*'
      kind: '*'
  namespaceResourceWhitelist:
    - group: '*'
      kind: '*'
```

```yaml
# petclinic-application.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: petclinic
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: petclinic-project
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/Mahmoud-Medany/pet-clinic.git
    targetRevision: main
    path: .
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: petclinic
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=true
```

```bash
kubectl apply -f petclinic-project.yaml
kubectl apply -f petclinic-application.yaml
```

ArgoCD now watches `app-k8s.yaml` on `main` and auto-syncs the cluster whenever
Jenkins commits a new image tag — completing the GitOps loop.

---

## 7. The Jenkinsfile (Sonar → Kaniko build/push → Trivy scan → manifest bump)

```groovy
pipeline {
  agent {
    kubernetes {
      yaml '''
        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Pod
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: kaniko
            image: gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor:debug
            command: ["sleep"]
            args: ["9999999"]
            volumeMounts:
              - name: docker-config
                mountPath: /kaniko/.docker
          - name: kubectl
            image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
            command: ["sleep"]
            args: ["9999999"]
          - name: sonar-scanner
            image: sonarsource/sonar-scanner-cli:latest
            command: ["sleep"]
            args: ["9999999"]
          - name: trivy
            image: aquasec/trivy:latest
            command: ["sleep"]
            args: ["9999999"]
          volumes:
            - name: docker-config
              secret:
                secretName: dockerhub-cred
                items:
                  - key: .dockerconfigjson
                    path: config.json
      '''
    }
  }

  triggers {
    pollSCM('H/1 * * * *')
  }

  environment {
    IMAGE_NAME = "medanyyy/pet-clinic"
    IMAGE_TAG  = "${env.BUILD_NUMBER}-${env.GIT_COMMIT.take(7)}"
  }

  stages {

    stage('Checkout') {
      steps {
        git url: 'https://github.com/Mahmoud-Medany/pet-clinic.git', branch: 'main'
      }
    }

    stage('SonarQube Analysis') {
      steps {
        container('sonar-scanner') {
          withSonarQubeEnv('sonarqube') {
            sh """
              sonar-scanner \
                -Dsonar.projectKey=pet-clinic \
                -Dsonar.sources=. \
                -Dsonar.host.url=http://sonarqube.petclinic.svc.cluster.local:9000
            """
          }
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Quality Gate') {
      steps {
        timeout(time: 5, unit: 'MINUTES') {
          waitForQualityGate abortPipeline: true
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Build & Push Image') {
      steps {
        container('kaniko') {
          sh """
            /kaniko/executor \
              --context=`pwd` \
              --destination=${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG} \
              --destination=${IMAGE_NAME}:latest
          """
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Trivy Image Scan') {
      steps {
        container('trivy') {
          sh """
            trivy image \
              --severity HIGH,CRITICAL \
              --exit-code 1 \
              --no-progress \
              ${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}
          """
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Update Deploy Manifest') {
      steps {
        sh """
          sed -i "s|image: ${IMAGE_NAME}:.*|image: ${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}|" app-k8s.yaml
        """
      }
    }

    stage('Commit Updated Manifest') {
      steps {
        withCredentials([usernamePassword(credentialsId: 'github-cred', usernameVariable: 'GIT_USER', passwordVariable: 'GIT_PASS')]) {
          sh """
            git config user.email "mahmmoudmedany213@gmail.com"
            git config user.name "Mahmoud-Medany"
            git add app-k8s.yaml
            git commit -m "ci: bump image to ${IMAGE_TAG}"
            git push https://${GIT_USER}:${GIT_PASS}@github.com/Mahmoud-Medany/pet-clinic.git HEAD:main
          """
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Notes:**
- `Deploy to Cluster` (`kubectl set image ...`) is intentionally **removed** — ArgoCD
  now owns deployment. Jenkins only builds, scans, and updates the manifest in git.
- `Trivy Image Scan` runs with `--exit-code 1` on HIGH/CRITICAL findings, which
  **fails the build** and blocks the manifest bump/ArgoCD sync if the image has
  serious vulnerabilities. Drop `--exit-code 1` (or lower severity threshold) if you
  want scanning to be informational only, not a gate.
- `pollSCM('H/1 * * * *')` polls the repo every minute — no public webhook needed
  since the kind cluster isn't internet-reachable. The trigger only activates
  **after the first successful manual run** ("Build Now").

Create the Jenkins job: **New Item → Pipeline → Pipeline script from SCM → Git →**
point at the repo, script path `Jenkinsfile`. Run it once manually to register the
poll trigger, then every push to `main` triggers a build within ~1 minute.

---

## 8. Verify the full loop

```bash
kubectl get pods -n petclinic -w
kubectl get application petclinic -n argocd
```

Push a code change → Jenkins polls, builds, Sonar-gates, Trivy-scans, pushes image,
bumps `app-k8s.yaml`, commits → ArgoCD detects the new commit and auto-syncs →
`petclinic` Deployment rolls out the new image.

---

## 9. Troubleshooting — problems hit during this build, and fixes

### 9.1 Ephemeral Jenkins (Job/Pod) vs UI plugin installs
**Symptom:** installing a plugin from the Jenkins UI killed the pod and it never
came back.
**Cause:** UI plugin installs trigger a Jenkins restart. The original setup used a
bare `Pod`/`Job` with `restartPolicy: Never` and `emptyDir` — by design, ephemeral
and non-recoverable. A restart = the container exits = nothing recreates it, and
even if something did, `emptyDir` wipes all state.
**Fix:** switched to a **StatefulSet + PersistentVolumeClaim** (Section 3) — restarts
recreate the pod, and `/var/jenkins_home` persists across them.

### 9.2 `Invalid agent type "kubernetes"` / `No Kubernetes cloud was found`
**Symptom:** Jenkinsfile using `agent { kubernetes { ... } }` failed to compile, or
later failed with "No Kubernetes cloud was found."
**Cause:** the `kubernetes` plugin (not just its dependencies `kubernetes-cli`,
`kubernetes-client-api`, `kubernetes-credentials`) wasn't actually installed — the
initContainer's `--plugins` list was missing the exact plugin ID, or it silently
failed to resolve. Verified with:
```bash
kubectl exec -n petclinic -it <jenkins-pod> -- ls /var/jenkins_home/plugins | grep -i kubernetes
```
**Fix:** ensure `kubernetes` (not just `kubernetes-cli`) is explicitly listed in the
initContainer plugin list, and configure a **Kubernetes Cloud** under Manage
Jenkins → Clouds (Section 3.1) — the plugin alone isn't enough, Jenkins also needs a
registered cloud target.

### 9.3 Kaniko `UNAUTHORIZED` pushing to DockerHub
**Symptom:**
```
error checking push permissions ... UNAUTHORIZED: authentication required
```
**Cause #1:** `kubectl create secret docker-registry` stores creds under key
`.dockerconfigjson`, but Kaniko expects a file literally named `config.json` at the
mount path. **Fix:** remap the key on mount:
```yaml
volumes:
  - name: docker-config
    secret:
      secretName: dockerhub-cred
      items:
        - key: .dockerconfigjson
          path: config.json
```
**Cause #2:** `incorrect username or password` / `insufficient scopes` — the
DockerHub access token was **Read-only**, not **Read & Write**. Push checks need
write scope explicitly.
**Cause #3:** a stray `>` character got appended to the token (`...0TU%E2%89%A0`)
from a corrupted PowerShell multi-line paste (backtick continuation mangled the
string) — always create/verify secrets as a **single-line** command, then decode
and eyeball the result:
```powershell
$json = kubectl get secret dockerhub-cred -n petclinic -o jsonpath='{.data.\.dockerconfigjson}'
[System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String($json))
```
**Isolated auth test** (proves token validity independent of the cluster):
```powershell
$user = "medanyyy"; $token = "<token>"
$pair = "$user`:$token"
$b64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($pair))
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://auth.docker.io/token?service=registry.docker.io&scope=repository:medanyyy/pet-clinic:pull,push" -Headers @{ Authorization = "Basic $b64" }
```
A JSON object with a `token` field = success.

### 9.4 ArgoCD `namespace argocd is not permitted in project 'petclinic-project'`
**Symptom:** shown in the ArgoCD UI screenshot-equivalent output — the
`Application`/`AppProject` manifests themselves got committed into the same repo
path (`.`) that the `petclinic` Application was syncing, so ArgoCD tried to
self-manage its own objects into the `argocd` namespace using a project scoped only
to `petclinic`.
**Fix:** keep ArgoCD's own `Application`/`AppProject` YAML **out of the synced app
repo** — apply them directly with `kubectl apply`, not through GitOps (Section 6.1).

### 9.5 Node `Insufficient memory` / cluster crash (`Unable to connect to the server: EOF`)
**Symptom:** SonarQube pod stuck `Pending` with `0/1 nodes are available: 1
Insufficient memory`, and later `kubectl` couldn't reach the API server at all —
the kind control-plane container was crash-looping with:
```
sed: couldn't flush stdout: Device or resource busy
```
**Cause:** the single kind node (default ~3.7–4GB Docker Desktop allocation) was
running Jenkins + 2× petclinic replicas + ArgoCD + SonarQube simultaneously,
pushing memory to 96%+ committed. A duplicate SonarQube ReplicaSet (old + new spec
both running) made it worse. The node's cgroup state eventually corrupted under
OOM pressure and the control-plane container stopped recovering.
**Fix:**
1. Clean up duplicate/stale pods:
   ```powershell
   kubectl get pods -A -o=custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,MEM-REQ:.spec.containers[*].resources.requests.memory
   kubectl delete pod <stale-pod> -n <ns>
   ```
2. If the control-plane container is already crash-looping, it will not recover —
   delete and recreate the cluster:
   ```powershell
   kind delete cluster
   ```
3. **Increase Docker Desktop memory** (Settings → Resources → Advanced → 6–8GB+) →
   Apply & Restart.
4. Recreate the cluster and reapply every manifest from scratch (namespace, RBAC,
   Jenkins, SonarQube, ArgoCD, app):
   ```powershell
   kind create cluster
   kubectl apply -f namespace.yaml
   kubectl apply -f jenkins-rbac.yaml
   kubectl apply -f jenkins-statefulset.yaml
   kubectl apply -f app-k8s.yaml
   kubectl apply -f sonarqube.yaml
   kubectl create namespace argocd
   kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/install.yaml
   kubectl apply -f petclinic-project.yaml
   kubectl apply -f petclinic-application.yaml
   ```

### 9.6 `serviceaccount "jenkins" not found`
**Symptom:** StatefulSet stuck at `0 total` replicas with
`FailedCreate ... error looking up service account petclinic/jenkins: serviceaccount "jenkins" not found`.
**Cause:** the RBAC manifest (ServiceAccount/Role/RoleBinding) was never (re)applied
in the current cluster — often after a cluster recreate or accidental cleanup.
**Fix:** reapply `jenkins-rbac.yaml` (Section 3), then confirm:
```bash
kubectl get serviceaccount jenkins -n petclinic
```

### 9.7 Groovy `expecting '}', found ''`
**Symptom:** Jenkinsfile failed to parse with a brace-count error at the end of the
file.
**Cause:** manually editing/removing a stage (e.g. deleting `Deploy to Cluster`)
also deleted the closing braces that belonged to the surrounding `stages { }` and
`pipeline { }` blocks.
**Fix:** every `stage(...) { steps { ... } }` needs its own closing brace, and the
whole file needs a final `}` for `stages` and one more for `pipeline`. Use an
editor with bracket-matching (VS Code) to verify brace balance before pushing.

### 9.8 Credential hygiene
Multiple DockerHub tokens and one GitHub PAT were pasted directly into this
conversation over the course of the build. **Every one of them had to be treated as
compromised and rotated immediately** — DockerHub access tokens
(Account Settings → Security) and the GitHub PAT
(Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens). Credentials should only
ever be typed into a local terminal or pasted into `kubectl create secret` /
Jenkins' credential store UI — never into chat, tickets, or shared docs.

---

## Screenshots from this build

**Jenkins dashboard — pipeline running, Kubernetes Cloud agent provisioning a build
pod (`pet-clinic-8-nx4np-f7fts-1kb3l`), executing `Build & Push Image`:**

![Jenkins dashboard showing pet-clinic pipeline with an active Kubernetes agent build](data:image/png;base64,)

**VS Code Kubernetes extension — cluster view showing the final resource layout:
`petclinic` Deployment (2 replicas), `jenkins` StatefulSet (`jenkins-0`), and the
ephemeral Kaniko build agent pod (`pet-clinic-8-nx4np-f7fts-1kb3l`) alongside the
`.dockerignore` correctly excluding `k8s/`, `Dockerfile`, and other non-build
context from the image:**

![VS Code Kubernetes cluster explorer showing petclinic deployment, jenkins statefulset, and build agent pod](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Summary — final architecture

| Component | Type | Namespace | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `petclinic` | Deployment (2 replicas) | `petclinic` | The app itself |
| `jenkins` | StatefulSet + PVC | `petclinic` | CI: build, test, scan, bump manifest |
| `sonarqube` | Deployment + PVC | `petclinic` | Static code analysis / quality gate |
| Kaniko / kubectl / sonar-scanner / trivy | ephemeral agent pods | `petclinic` | Spawned per-build by Jenkins' Kubernetes Cloud, torn down after |
| ArgoCD (`argocd-server`, `repo-server`, etc.) | Deployments/StatefulSet | `argocd` | GitOps continuous deployment |
| `dockerhub-cred` | Secret | `petclinic` | Kaniko push credentials |
| `github-cred` | Jenkins credential | — | Push manifest bumps back to GitHub |
